examples: He's a retired lieutenant colonel and a bit of a martinet.
They discover that the new teacher is a martinet.
The martinet governed his classroom with procrustean discipline, assigning a week's detention to all offenders, no matter what the offense.
Newspapers indignantly portrayed him as a martinet who had made himself prime minister in all but name.
Here he dresses the part of a colonial martinet: tight jackets, jodhpurs, riding boots, and even affects a cigarette holder.
